items.title: First case of deadly H5 bird flu confirmed in Australia

children.content: The virus, found in a migratory bird in Esperance, raises concerns for the poultry industry, despite Australia having spent years preparing for such a biosecurity breach.

articles.title: Why Australia's first H5 bird flu case is 'deeply concerning'

children.content: Over recent years avian influenza has made its way across the globe. Now Australia has its first case. Here's what we know.

image.altText: two people in personal protection suits examine a a dead animal on a beach

gallery.altText: a scientist hold up a test tube that says 'bird flu' on it

thumbnail.altText: a scientist hold up a test tube that says 'bird flu' on it

items.title: Why is your phone going off? Don't be alarmed, it's a warning system test

image.altText: A phone notification with the words 'Priority AusAlert'.

children.content: This week, phones in some regional towns have received a warning text alert and emitted an alarm as part of national warning system testing. Next month, your phone could receive it too.

items.title: Sixteen years after Josh was murdered, will the case ever be solved?

children.content: The grieving mother of Josh Warneke, who was found dead on the side of a road in WA's remote Kimberley, has faced a long battle for answers. But how likely is it the 21-year-old's killer will be brought to justice?

gallery.altText: Image of a photograph of Josh Warneke placed in front of a memorial in Broome, Western Australia.

thumbnail.altText: Image of a photograph of Josh Warneke placed in front of a memorial in Broome, Western Australia.

items.title: 'Not about mass surveillance': WA Police first in country to use live facial recognition

children.content: WA Police are set to become the first in the country to trial live facial recognition of crowds, describing the technology as safer than standard CCTV cameras.

image.altText: A close-up shot of two live facial recognition cameras on top of a pole.

items.title: WA government wants federal help with Collie's coal transition

children.content: The state government has already tipped $700 million into the transition of the WA town of Collie away from coal. Now, the Cook government wants tens of millions of federal dollars to finish the job.

image.altText: sEVERAL COAL WORKERS HOLDING BLACK SIGNS AND A WHITE BANNER PROTESTING THE COAL TRANSITION

items.title: 'Miscarriage of justice' leads to retrial for parents jailed for starving daughter

children.content: The WA Court of Appeal has overturned the guilty verdicts of Perth parents who starved their daughter after ruling that the judge's conduct in the jury trial amounted to a "miscarriage of justice".

gallery.altText: A picture of a girl in a yellow and white tutu posing.

thumbnail.altText: A picture of a girl in a yellow and white tutu posing.

items.title: WA mum warns changes to NDIS could take choices away from her son

children.content: Annette Nicholas and her husband last year became disability support workers to share their "lived experience" of raising 15-year-old son Tre, who is intellectually disabled and also lives with autism.

gallery.altText: A mother and son walking in a park.
A teenage boy sitting down with a turkey behind fence.
Mother and son walking together in park

thumbnail.altText: A mother and son walking in a park.
A teenage boy sitting down with a turkey behind fence.
Mother and son walking together in park

items.title: Applause for Modra as Ricciuto describes optimism following surgery

children.content: Adelaide Crows fans clap at the sixth minute of the team's match against Melbourne to wish former number-six player Tony Modra well in his recovery from surgery following a serious truck crash.

image.altText: A man wearing a T-shirt with Tony Modra's picture on it under a blue jacket

items.title: What does it mean for a nation to be a 'monoculture'?

children.content: Pauline Hanson has called for Australia to reject multiculturalism and be "monocultural". So what does it involve and are there any nations overseas that show what it might look like in Australia?

image.altText: People walk across a zebra crossing in a city with billboards in Japanese in the background.

items.title: Government says possum bridges successful, but skeptics question data

children.content: The WA government has released night-vision images showing scores of native wildlife are using rope bridges and underpasses along a $1.46 billion highway in the South West region, but not everyone is convinced.

gallery.altText: A night vision image of two ringtail possums crawling across a rope bridge toward the camera.
A night vision image of three possums on a rope bridge with three branches in the beackground
A baby ringtail possum being syringe fed on a carers arm.

thumbnail.altText: A night vision image of two ringtail possums crawling across a rope bridge toward the camera.
A night vision image of three possums on a rope bridge with three branches in the beackground
A baby ringtail possum being syringe fed on a carers arm.

items.title: If you didn't do it at 40, do it at 60

children.content: For some women, the act of asking, "what might still be possible" — of refusing to accept that the most meaningful chapters are already behind them — might be its own kind of resistance.

image.altText: Tilda Swinton poses on a red carpet wearing a red long sleeve dress and gold choker

avatar.alt: Virginia Trioli in a stylish suit on a set with lighting behind her

items.title: Twin brothers born deaf hear their mum's voice for first time after epic surgery

children.content: An Australian-first eight-hour surgical feat had one main purpose — for Artie and Jack to hear their mum's voice at the same time.

image.altText: Twin boys sitting in high chair smiling.

items.title: Hantavirus ship passenger recounts 'huge shock' as quarantine nears end

children.content: The first Australian to speak publicly about being onboard the MV Hondius shares his experience of the deadly outbreak on the cruise ship, as he prepares to leave a Perth quarantine facility and return home to his family.

gallery.altText: An elderly man in personal protection gear stands in the lobby of a cruise ship with a map on the wall
A passenger on board the cruise ship MV Hondius

thumbnail.altText: An elderly man in personal protection gear stands in the lobby of a cruise ship with a map on the wall
A passenger on board the cruise ship MV Hondius

items.title: A Sydney startup built an AI porn empire. There are serious questions about child abuse material

children.content: The ABC traced a Sydney startup to one of the world's biggest AI pornography sites and found what experts say is child abuse material.

image.altText: A montage of profile photos of characters with blurred faces and age estiamtes beside them.

items.title: Melbourne woman suffers acute liver failure after using knock-off drug

children.content: Megan Hancocks bought a drug labelled as the peptide compound retatrutide in cash from a beautician. Within a week of her second dose, she was in acute liver failure.

image.altText: A woman lies in a hospital bed with her eyes closed.

items.title: Pensioner living in car has 'lost faith' in system as he waits for house

children.content: Despite upcoming changes from the WA government to overhaul the housing waitlist, a 68-year-old living in his car says he has lost faith in the system as an advocate calls for faster action.

gallery.altText: Mark Unsworth in car looks to camera.
Mark Unsworth looks out to the skyline above the ocean.
Mark Unsworth in car looks away from the camera from his car seat.

thumbnail.altText: Mark Unsworth in car looks to camera.
Mark Unsworth looks out to the skyline above the ocean.
Mark Unsworth in car looks away from the camera from his car seat.

avatar.alt: A blonde woman named Courtney Withers stands wearing a blazer looking serious.

items.title: Zombie director Yeon Sang-ho says AI wants to 'fold' us into one

children.content: Train to Busan director Yeon Sang-ho is ringing the warning bell over AI's potential to strip humans of their individuality with his new zombie film Colony.

image.altText: Colony director and writer Yeon Sang-ho.

items.title: Renshaw shines with bat and ball as Aussies seal Bangladesh T20 series

children.content: Matt Renshaw blasted a career-best 89 not out as Australia notched a series-clinching six-run victory over Bangladesh in the second T20 of their three-match series.

image.altText: Batter plays a shot into the offside with an open bat

items.title: Hawks break drought at Carrara, winning tough encounter against Suns

children.content: The Hawks win at Carrara for the first time since 2014, after a tough, physical encounter against the Suns.

image.altText: A grinning Hawthorn AFL player clenches his fist in celebration after a goal.

items.title: An orange bulldozer is ploughing through the political arena

children.content: Four years ago, Australians decided they didn't want a bulldozer for a prime minister. But now if Gina Rinehart has her way, a big orange one will be ploughing her way through the political arena.

items.title: One dead after huge fire at Dominican beach resort

children.content: A woman has been killed and nearly 1,700 tourists evacuated due to a large fire at a hotel in the Dominican beach resort town of Bayahibe.

image.altText: Drone vision of thick pluming smoke and flames over a beachside resort in the Dominican Republic.

gallery.altText: A resort on fire next to bright blue water.

thumbnail.altText: A resort on fire next to bright blue water.
